UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information, excluding authentication credentials. Each user can own multiple projects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name or display name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a user's project, acting as a container for datasets, data items, and configurations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

description.description: A brief description of the project.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the project was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the project was last updated.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that owns this project. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Project)

DataSet.title: DataSet
DataSet.description: Represents a collection of structured data imported into a specific project, often originating from a single source like a CSV or JSON file.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DataSet entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the data set (e.g., 'Customer Data', 'Sales Records').

description.description: A brief description of the data set's content.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this data set belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N DataSet)

importTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the data set was imported.

ProjectDataItem.title: ProjectDataItem
ProjectDataItem.description: Represents a single record or item of data within a DataSet, containing the actual structured data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProjectDataItem entity.

dataSetId.description: Reference to the DataSet this data item belongs to. (Relationship: DataSet 1:N ProjectDataItem)

data.description: The actual structured data for this item, stored as a JSON string to allow flexible schema.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the data item was created or imported.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the data item was last updated.

ViewConfiguration.title: ViewConfiguration
ViewConfiguration.description: Stores user-defined preferences for how data within a project or dataset should be visualized (e.g., table layout, sorting, filtering).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ViewConfiguration entity.

name.description: A user-friendly name for this view configuration.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this view configuration applies to. (Relationship: Project 1:N ViewConfiguration)

dataSetId.description: Optional reference to a specific DataSet if the view is data-set-specific. (Relationship: DataSet 1:N ViewConfiguration)

layoutType.description: The type of layout used for this view (e.g., 'table', 'cards', 'grid').

configurationJson.description: A JSON string containing the detailed configuration for the view, including sorting, filtering, visible columns, etc.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the view configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the view configuration was last updated.

AISuggestion.title: AISuggestion
AISuggestion.description: Stores suggestions generated by the AI tool, such as optimal organizational structures or identified key entities for a project or dataset.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AISuggestion entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this AI suggestion is related to. (Relationship: Project 1:N AISuggestion)

dataSetId.description: Optional reference to a specific DataSet if the suggestion is dataset-specific. (Relationship: DataSet 1:N AISuggestion)

suggestionType.description: The type of suggestion provided by the AI (e.g., 'schema_recommendation', 'entity_identification', 'data_cleaning').

suggestionJson.description: A JSON string containing the detailed suggestion content, which could be a proposed schema, identified entities, or other analytical insights.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the AI suggestion was generated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. The 'userProfileId' must match the authenticated user's UID (request.auth.uid) for ownership.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Projects owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' for authorization independence and efficient list queries.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the project.

definition.description: Collections of structured data belonging to a specific project. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' and 'projectId' for authorization independence and list queries.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent project.

params.name: dataSetId
params.description: The unique ID of the data set.

definition.description: Individual data records within a specific data set. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId', 'projectId', and 'dataSetId' for authorization independence and list queries.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the grand-parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent project.

params.name: dataSetId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent data set.

params.name: dataItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the data item.

definition.description: User-defined display preferences for project data. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' and 'projectId' for authorization independence and list queries.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent project.

params.name: viewConfigurationId
params.description: The unique ID of the view configuration.

definition.description: AI-generated suggestions related to a project or its datasets. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' and 'projectId' for authorization independence and list queries.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent project.

params.name: aiSuggestionId
params.description: The unique ID of the AI suggestion.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs) by leveraging a strictly hierarchical path structure combined with strategic denormalization. All user-specific data is rooted under a top-level `/users/{userProfileId}` collection, where `{userProfileId}` directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication `request.auth.uid`. This path-based ownership ensures that all direct access to a user's data collection inherently filters by their authenticated ID, making `list` operations secure by default (QAPs).

To achieve Authorization Independence for nested resources, key authorization attributes are denormalized into subcollection documents. For example:

1.  **Project Ownership**: A `Project` document, located at `/users/{userProfileId}/projects/{projectId}`, will explicitly store `userProfileId` (referencing its owner). This allows rules for `projects` to simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId` without needing to `get()` the parent `UserProfile` document.

2.  **DataSet, ViewConfiguration, AISuggestion Ownership**: These entities are direct children of a `Project`. Their paths are `.../projects/{projectId}/dataSets/{dataSetId}`, `.../viewConfigurations/{viewConfigurationId}`, and `.../aiSuggestions/{aiSuggestionId}` respectively. Each document within these subcollections will denormalize both the `userProfileId` (from the grand-parent UserProfile) and the `projectId` (from the parent Project). This allows security rules for these documents to perform checks like `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId && request.auth.uid == get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(resource.data.userProfileId)/projects/$(resource.data.projectId)).data.userProfileId` (though this specific `get` is avoided by denormalizing `userProfileId` into the child itself). The actual rule becomes `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId && resource.data.projectId == projectId` where `projectId` is the path wildcard, ensuring complete authorization context within the document or its immediate path.

3.  **ProjectDataItem Ownership**: `ProjectDataItem` documents, located at `.../dataSets/{dataSetId}/dataItems/{dataItemId}`, will denormalize `userProfileId`, `projectId`, and `dataSetId`. This ensures that access rules for a `ProjectDataItem` can be evaluated purely based on its own `resource.data` and the path wildcards, e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId && resource.data.projectId == projectId && resource.data.dataSetId == dataSetId`.

This design completely eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, supports atomic creation of related documents, and provides clear, debuggable authorization logic. The structural segregation principle is followed by ensuring all documents within a given collection (e.g., all documents in `/users/{userProfileId}/projects`) share the same security posture – they are owned by the `userProfileId` in the path.
